entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The host plant used in the study to evaluate the effects of Bt toxins and resistance on pests and parasitoids

entities.name: BACILLUS THURINGIENSIS
entities.description: A bacterium used as a source of toxin genes for pest control

entities.name: TOXIN GENES
entities.description: Genes from Bacillus thuringiensis expressed in chickpea to control pod borers

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: The pod borer pest that feeds on chickpea

entities.name: CAMPOLETIS CHLORIDEAE
entities.description: A larval parasitoid of Helicoverpa armigera

entities.name: BT SPRAY
entities.description: The application of Bacillus thuringiensis as a spray on chickpea genotypes

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: The ability of certain chickpea genotypes to reduce leaf feeding, survival, and development of H. armigera

entities.name: TRITROPHIC INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The complex interactions between the plant (chickpea), the herbivore (H. armigera), and the parasitoid (C. chlorideae)

entities.name: BT-TRANSGENIC CHICKPEA
entities.description: Chickpea engineered to express Bt toxins for sustainable pest management

entities.name: BT INTOXICATION
entities.description: The state of H. armigera larvae after consuming Bt toxins

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The long-term goal of using Bt-transgenic crops for efficient and environmentally friendly agriculture
